design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore interconnected islands, collect inventory items, solve environmental puzzles through inventory combination and NPC interaction to progress the narrative toward finding Big Whoop
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Pursuit narrative: LeChuck's escalating threat and resurrection creates constant pressure while Guybrush seeks map pieces across islands; emotional tension between protagonist and estranged love interest Elaine
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a clever pirate adventurer who solves problems through wit, charm, and creative item usage rather than combat; navigate Caribbean islands and uncover legendary treasure
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Puzzle-first design that privileges logical problem-solving and inventory experimentation over action mechanics; accessibility philosophy offering difficulty choice (standard vs 'Monkey 2 Lite' stripped mode); emphasis on narrative comedy and character writing over challenge; rejection of failure states—players cannot die or lose, only get stuck

difficulty.lives_system: None; no death mechanic. Game designed so player cannot fail or lose
difficulty.checkpointing: Free-form progression with ability to revisit all islands at will; no hard checkpoints, but puzzle solutions are permanent once completed
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: player can select 'Monkey 2 Lite' difficulty mode before game start which bypasses many puzzles entirely for streamlined experience. Standard mode includes full puzzle set. Players can also abandon puzzle attempts and explore other content without penalty

progression.upgrade_system: Inventory accumulation system where collected items become tools for puzzle solving; no RPG-style stats or mechanical upgrades
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-gated progression: collecting four map pieces unlocks access to final island (Dinky Island) and true ending. Island access is controlled by narrative events (embargo lifted, ship chartered, etc.). Some NPCs and locations become available as story progresses
replayability_hooks: Difficulty selection (standard vs Lite mode) provides alternate experience
Comedy writing and character interactions encourage replaying for narrative content
Multiple valid puzzle solutions in some scenarios
Post-game cliffhanger ending invites curiosity about sequel

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling: islands populated with distinct NPCs (Governor Phatt, Stan the salesman, Herman Toothrot, Voodoo Lady) that flesh out world through dialogue
Repeated NPC encounters across multiple islands establish persistent world and character arcs (Stan's career change from ship sales to coffin sales; Elaine's emotional arc from breakup to reunion)
Location design conveys history: Dinky Island's castaway hermit, underground facility reveal, skeleton remains of Guybrush's parents
Dialogue-heavy exposition: characters deliver plot information, worldbuilding, and comedy through verbal commands and conversation trees
Interconnected geography: revisitable locations create sense of coherent world rather than isolated levels
Voodoo magic system embedded in lore (Voodoo Lady, voodoo dolls) naturalizes puzzle logic within world

boss_design.philosophy: LeChuck functions as a narrative antagonist rather than direct combat opponent. Conflicts resolved through environmental puzzle-solving (voodoo doll creation) and escape sequences rather than boss fights. Focus on character rivalry and story tension over mechanical challenge
notable_bosses: LeChuck (zombie pirate antagonist): encountered multiple times across game, progressively more threatening; final confrontation involves elaborate death-trap puzzle and voodoo doll torture mechanic rather than direct combat

faction_indicators: Pirate identity and allegiances (Guybrush as pirate protagonist)
Voodoo magic practitioners (Voodoo Lady as mentor figure)
Treasure hunters seeking Big Whoop legend
Island governance (Governor Phatt's authority, LeChuck's fortress control)
danger_signals: Largo LaGrande's embargo trapping people on Scabb Island
Bounty placed on Guybrush's head creating external threat
Imprisonment on Phatt Island by Governor's order
Elaborate death-trap scenarios designed by LeChuck
environmental_storytelling: International House of Mojo in swamp: mystical refuge outside normal society
LeChuck's island fortress: visual representation of antagonist's power and control
Underground facility beneath Big Whoop: reveals larger world structure (Mêlée Island connection, lost parents area, E-ticket)
Multi-island geography: establishes scale of adventure and isolation of locations
NPC-specific locations (Stan's coffin shop, Wally's cartography office, Herman's castaway camp) personalize world through character spaces
